get.description: Lightweight liveness probe endpoint.
Alias liveness probe endpoint for platform compatibility.
Readiness probe endpoint for service orchestration checks.

post.description: Resolve caller identity from auth headers and return the canonical user profile. This endpoint does not accept a request body.

200.description: Authenticated user profile resolved from token claims.

401.description: Caller is not authenticated as a user actor.

get.description:
Token-authenticated liveness probe for agent API clients.

Use this endpoint when the caller needs to verify both service availability and agent-token validity in one request.

422.description: Validation Error

x-when-to-use: Verify agent token validity before entering an automation loop
Confirm agent API availability with caller identity context
x-when-not-to-use: General infrastructure liveness checks that do not require auth context
Task, board, or messaging workflow actions
x-prerequisites: Authenticated agent token via X-Agent-Token header
x-side-effects: May refresh agent last-seen presence metadata via auth middleware
x-negative-guidance: Do not parse this response as an array.
Do not use this endpoint for task routing decisions.
x-routing-policy: Use this as the first probe for agent-scoped automation health.
Use /healthz only for unauthenticated service-level liveness checks.

input.intent: agent startup probe with token verification
platform-level probe with no agent token

get.summary: List boards visible to the caller
get.description:
Return boards the authenticated agent can access.

Use this as a discovery step before board-scoped operations.

422.description: Validation Error

x-when-to-use: Discover boards available to the current agent
Build a board selection list before read/write operations
x-when-not-to-use: Use direct board-id endpoints when the target board is already known
Use task-only views when board context is not needed
x-negative-guidance: Do not use as a task mutation mechanism.
Do not treat this as a strict inventory cache endpoint.
x-routing-policy: Use for board discovery before board-scoped actions.
Fallback to board-specific fetch or task routes once target is known.

input.intent: agent needs boards to plan next actions
board target is known

get.description:
Read a single board entity if it is visible to the authenticated agent.

Use for targeted planning and routing decisions.

x-when-to-use: Resolve board metadata before creating or updating board tasks

x-when-not-to-use: Bulk discovery of all accessible boards
Task list mutation workflows without board context
x-prerequisites: Target board id must be accessible
x-negative-guidance: Do not call for creating or mutating board fields.
Do not use when board_id is unknown; discover first.
x-routing-policy: Use when a specific board id is known and validation of scope is needed.
Use task list endpoints for repeated board-scoped task discovery.

input.intent: agent needs full board context for planning

get.description:

Use task or direct nudge endpoints for one-off actor targeting.

get.description:
Return a lightweight work snapshot for an agent.

Answers "should this agent wake up?" without reasoning, memory pulls,
or assist-mode overhead.  Includes busy-gating: if the agent already
has a running run, should_wake is false.

Authorization:
- Agent token: can read only own snapshot
- Board lead: can read snapshots of team agents on same board
- Board admin/owner: can read snapshots of agents on their board
- Org admin: full access

post.description:
Repair agent auth sync for a drifted agent.

Healing/idempotent operation — resets to a known good state:
- legacy_hash: rollback any stale pending, start fresh migration,
  reprovision with new pending token, reset + wake
- signed: rollback any stale pending, reprovision with current active
  token (does NOT bump version), reset + wake

422.description: Request payload failed schema or field validation.

post.description:
Rotate agent auth token via staged rotation.

Creates a new pending token version, reprovisions templates,
resets session, and wakes the agent. The old token remains valid
until the first successful heartbeat with the new token.

x-when-not-to-use: Use a more specific endpoint when targeting a single actor or broadcast scope.

post.description:

When tags/mentions indicate chat or broadcast intent, eligible agents in the
linked group are notified.

422.description: Request payload failed schema or field validation.

x-when-to-use: Persist shared group memory for a linked group from board context.
Broadcast updates/messages to group-linked agents when chat or mention intent is present.
x-when-not-to-use: Use a more specific endpoint when targeting a single actor or broadcast scope.
x-side-effects: Persist new group-memory entries with optional agent notification dispatch.
x-negative-guidance: Do not use as a replacement for direct task-specific commentary.
Do not assume infinite retention when group storage policies apply.

Prefer direct board memory endpoints for board-local persistence.

input.intent: share coordination signal in group memory

422.description: Request payload failed schema or field validation.

x-when-not-to-use: Use a more specific endpoint when targeting a single actor or broadcast scope.
x-side-effects: No persisted side effects, streaming updates are read-only.
x-negative-guidance: Do not use as a replacement for direct task-specific commentary.
Do not assume infinite retention when group storage policies apply.
x-routing-policy: Use when coordinated decisions need continuous group context.
Prefer bounded history reads when a snapshot is sufficient.

board_id.title: Board Id
board_id.description: Target board id for this result.

lead_agent_id.title: Lead Agent Id
lead_agent_id.description: Resolved lead agent id for the target board.

error.title: Error
error.description: Failure reason if this board failed.

GatewayLeadBroadcastBoardResult.title: GatewayLeadBroadcastBoardResult
GatewayLeadBroadcastBoardResult.description: Per-board result entry for a lead broadcast operation.
GatewayLeadBroadcastBoardResult.x-interpretation: Use this result object as a transport status for one board.
x-when-not-to-use: Global summary checks should use parent broadcast response
x-when-to-use: Reading per-board outcomes for retries/follow-up workflows

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Broadcast intent. `question` asks for responses; `handoff` requests transfer.

correlation_id.title: Correlation Id
correlation_id.description: Optional correlation token shared with downstream handlers.

content.title: Content
content.description: Message content distributed to selected board leads.
examples: Board-wide incident: prioritize risk triage on task set 14.

board_ids.title: Board Ids
board_ids.description: Optional explicit list of board IDs; omit for lead-scoped defaults.

reply_tags.title: Reply Tags
reply_tags.description: Tags required by reply templates when each lead responds.

reply_source.title: Reply Source
reply_source.description: Reply destination key for broadcast responses.

GatewayLeadBroadcastRequest.title: GatewayLeadBroadcastRequest
GatewayLeadBroadcastRequest.description: Request payload for broadcasting a message to multiple board leads.
x-when-not-to-use: Single lead response required (use direct message)
Personalized board-level instruction from agent context
x-when-to-use: Multiple board leads need the same message
Coordinating cross-board operational alerts

failed.title: Failed
failed.description: Number of boards that failed messaging.

GatewayLeadBroadcastResponse.title: GatewayLeadBroadcastResponse
GatewayLeadBroadcastResponse.description: Aggregate response for a lead broadcast operation.
GatewayLeadBroadcastResponse.x-interpretation: Use sent/failed counters before considering retry logic.
x-when-not-to-use: Single-board directed lead message (use GatewayLeadMessageResponse)
x-when-to-use: Inspect final counters after attempting a multi-board send.

correlation_id.title: Correlation Id
correlation_id.description: Optional correlation token shared across upstream and downstream systems.

content.title: Content
content.description: Human-readable body sent to lead agents.
examples: Please triage the highest-priority blocker on board X.

reply_tags.title: Reply Tags
reply_tags.description: Tags required by reply templates when the lead responds.

reply_source.title: Reply Source
reply_source.description: Reply destination key for the orchestrator.

GatewayLeadMessageRequest.title: GatewayLeadMessageRequest
GatewayLeadMessageRequest.description: Request payload for sending a message to a board lead agent.
x-when-not-to-use: Broadcasting to many leads (use broadcast request)
Requesting end-user decisions (use ask-user request)
x-when-to-use: A board has an urgent tactical request that needs direct lead routing
You need a specific lead response before delegating work

lead_agent_id.title: Lead Agent Id
lead_agent_id.description: Resolved lead agent id when present.

lead_created.title: Lead Created
lead_created.description: Whether a lead fallback actor was created during routing.

GatewayLeadMessageResponse.title: GatewayLeadMessageResponse
GatewayLeadMessageResponse.description: Response payload for a lead-message dispatch attempt.
GatewayLeadMessageResponse.x-interpretation: Use to confirm handoff path and recipient lead context.
x-when-not-to-use: Broadcast outcomes (use GatewayLeadBroadcastResponse)
x-when-to-use: Confirm lead routing outcome for a direct message request.

correlation_id.title: Correlation Id
correlation_id.description: Optional correlation token for tracing request/response flow.

content.title: Content
content.description: Prompt that should be asked to the human.
examples: Can we proceed with the proposed vendor budget increase?

reply_tags.title: Reply Tags
reply_tags.description: Tags required for routing the user response.

reply_source.title: Reply Source
reply_source.description: Reply destination key for user confirmation loops.

Task flow requires preference confirmation or permission

board_id.title: Board Id

main_agent_id.title: Main Agent Id
